text: In which year did World War II begin?
explanation: World War II began on 1 September 1939 with Germany's invasion of Poland.

text: Who was the first human in space?
explanation: Yuri Gagarin made the first crewed spaceflight on 12 April 1961.

text: In which year did humans first land on the Moon?
explanation: The Apollo 11 crew landed on the Moon on 20 July 1969.

text: Which ancient civilization built the pyramids of Giza?
explanation: The Giza pyramids were built by the ancient Egyptians around 2500 BC.

text: Who was the first President of the United States?
explanation: George Washington became the first U.S. president in 1789.

text: In which year did the Soviet Union dissolve?
explanation: The Soviet Union officially ceased to exist in December 1991.

text: Who led Nazi Germany during World War II?
explanation: Adolf Hitler was the leader of Nazi Germany.

text: Which passenger liner sank in 1912 after hitting an iceberg?
explanation: The Titanic sank on the night of 15 April 1912 on its maiden voyage.

text: In which city is the ancient Colosseum located?
explanation: The Colosseum is an amphitheatre in Rome, built in the 1st century AD.

text: Who was the founder of Marxism and author of 'Das Kapital'?
explanation: Karl Marx wrote 'Das Kapital' and, with Engels, developed Marxism.

text: Which country did Queen Cleopatra rule?
explanation: Cleopatra VII was the last ruler of Hellenistic Egypt.

text: In which year did World War I begin?
explanation: World War I began in 1914 and lasted until 1918.

text: Who led the October Revolution of 1917 in Russia?
explanation: The Bolsheviks, led by Lenin, took power in the October Revolution of 1917.

text: What was the capital of the Byzantine Empire called?
explanation: The capital of Byzantium was Constantinople.

text: Who reached the Americas in 1492 by sailing west?
explanation: Christopher Columbus's expedition reached the Americas in 1492.

text: On which continent did the civilization of Ancient Egypt arise?
explanation: Ancient Egypt lay in north-eastern Africa along the Nile.

text: Who became the first Emperor of the French in 1804?
explanation: Napoleon Bonaparte crowned himself Emperor of the French in 1804.

text: Who was the first woman in space?
explanation: Valentina Tereshkova flew into space in 1963.

text: Which wall divided Germany's main city until 1989?
explanation: The Berlin Wall divided the city from 1961 to 1989.

text: Who was the last Russian emperor?
explanation: Nicholas II abdicated in 1917, the last Russian emperor.

text: In which year was the Battle of Kulikovo?
explanation: The Battle of Kulikovo between Dmitry Donskoy and Mamai took place in 1380.

text: Which prince converted Kievan Rus to Christianity in 988?
explanation: The Christianization of Rus took place under Prince Vladimir in 988.

text: Which battle is regarded as the turning point on the Eastern Front of WWII?
explanation: The Battle of Stalingrad (1942–1943) was the turning point of the war in the East.

text: In which year was the U.S. Declaration of Independence adopted?
explanation: The Declaration of Independence was adopted on 4 July 1776.

text: Which revolution began in France in 1789?
explanation: The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille.

text: Who was the first ruler to be crowned Tsar of Russia?
explanation: Ivan IV (the Terrible) was crowned Tsar in 1547, the first Russian tsar.

text: In which year did the Western Roman Empire fall?
explanation: The Western Roman Empire fell in 476 when Romulus Augustulus was deposed.

text: Which event marked the start of the German–Soviet war (the 'Great Patriotic War')?
options: Germany's invasion of the USSR on 22 June 1941
explanation: It began on 22 June 1941 with Germany's invasion of the USSR.

text: Who was the British Prime Minister during World War II?
explanation: Winston Churchill led the British government from 1940 to 1945.

text: What was Mikhail Gorbachev's policy of reform in the late 1980s called?
explanation: Perestroika was the reform programme launched by Gorbachev in 1985.

text: Which dynasty ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917?
explanation: The Romanov dynasty ruled Russia for over 300 years, from 1613 to 1917.

text: To which historical era does Leonardo da Vinci belong?
explanation: Leonardo da Vinci was one of the greatest masters of the Renaissance.

text: Who led the French invasion of Russia in 1812?
explanation: The Patriotic War of 1812 was fought against Napoleon's army.

text: Which Roman city was destroyed by the eruption of Vesuvius in AD 79?
explanation: The AD 79 eruption of Vesuvius buried Pompeii and Herculaneum.

text: Who was the first President of Russia?
explanation: Boris Yeltsin was elected the first President of Russia in 1991.

text: In which year did the American Civil War begin?
explanation: The American Civil War lasted from 1861 to 1865.

text: Who unified Germany in 1871 through a policy of 'blood and iron'?
explanation: Chancellor Otto von Bismarck united the German states into one empire in 1871.

text: Which peace treaty formally ended World War I?
explanation: The Treaty of Versailles in 1919 concluded World War I.

text: Who commanded the assault on Berlin in 1945 as head of the 1st Belorussian Front?
explanation: The Berlin operation was led by Marshal Georgy Zhukov.

text: Which ancient South American civilization built Machu Picchu?
explanation: Machu Picchu was built by the Inca Empire in the 15th century.

text: In which year was the Battle of Poltava?
explanation: The Battle of Poltava, Peter I's defeat of the Swedes, took place in 1709.

text: Which 1215 document limited the power of the English king?
explanation: Magna Carta was sealed by King John in 1215.

text: Who set out the heliocentric system of the world in a 1543 work?
explanation: Copernicus published his heliocentric model in 1543, the year of his death.

text: In which year did the Decembrist revolt take place?
explanation: The Decembrist revolt on Senate Square occurred on 14 December 1825.

text: Which battle of 1066 led to the Norman conquest of England?
explanation: At Hastings in 1066, William the Conqueror defeated the Anglo-Saxons.

text: Who was the last pharaoh of Hellenistic Egypt before Roman rule?
explanation: Cleopatra VII was the last ruler of the Ptolemaic dynasty.

text: In which year was serfdom abolished in Russia?
explanation: Serfdom was abolished by Alexander II's manifesto in 1861.

text: What is the period of mass executions in the French Revolution, linked to Robespierre, called?
explanation: The Reign of Terror of 1793–1794 was led by Maximilien Robespierre.

text: Which battle of 480 BC made 300 Spartans famous?
explanation: At Thermopylae, King Leonidas and his Spartans held off the Persians in 480 BC.

text: Who started the Reformation by posting 95 theses in 1517?
explanation: Martin Luther posted his 95 theses in Wittenberg in 1517.

text: In which year did Napoleon suffer his final defeat at Waterloo?
explanation: The Battle of Waterloo took place in 1815 and ended Napoleon's era.

text: Which empire fell with the capture of Constantinople in 1453?
explanation: In 1453 the Ottomans took Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire.

text: Who was the first tsar of the Romanov dynasty?
explanation: Michael Romanov was elected tsar in 1613.

text: Which 1648 peace ended the Thirty Years' War?
explanation: The Peace of Westphalia in 1648 ended the Thirty Years' War.

text: In which year did the storming of the Bastille take place?
explanation: The Bastille fell on 14 July 1789, the symbolic start of the French Revolution.

text: Which Mongol ruler founded the largest empire of the 13th century?
explanation: Genghis Khan united the Mongol tribes and built a vast empire in the early 13th century.

text: Which Russian prince defeated the Swedes in the Battle of the Neva in 1240?
explanation: For his victory on the Neva in 1240, Prince Alexander earned the name Nevsky.

text: In which year was the United Nations founded?
explanation: The UN was founded in 1945 after World War II.

text: Which general led the Carthaginians and crossed the Alps with elephants in the Second Punic War?
explanation: Hannibal Barca crossed the Alps and invaded Italy in 218 BC.

text: Who discovered the sea route to India around Africa in 1498?
explanation: The Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama reached India by sea around Africa in 1498.
